top_blockers: Real 0.197 focused before/after/delta performance artifacts are missing.

SQL parameter exact-key canonicalization is design-required but currently unsupported.

summary.biggest_residual_risk: Exact-key proof ownership is spread across selected access, access snapshots, input-resource summaries, admission, explain, and cache keys instead of one planner-owned artifact.

evidence: No cost-based optimizer, persisted-format, cursor-token, generated-model runtime fallback, or 0.198 API implementation found.

AccessPlan, AccessChoiceExplainSnapshot, PrimaryKeyInputResourceSummary, and admission together carry the proof facts.
blocking_issues: Strict design closeout requires a first-class artifact or an amended design that names the implemented artifact model.

evidence: plan_compare uses SchemaInfo::scalar_primary_key_name() and accepted field metadata.

SQL parameters fail before admission under current contract.

No complete zero-IO proof was found for all contradictory exact-key shapes.

evidence: SQL literal and fluent exact-key forms are covered.

findings.title: Canonicalization proof is implicit rather than the explicit design artifact
evidence: No PrimaryKeyCanonicalization enum found.
Proof facts are spread across AccessPlan, AccessChoiceExplainSnapshot, PrimaryKeyInputResourceSummary, access canonicalization, and admission.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a first-class planner-owned artifact or amend the design to declare the selected-access artifact model and add missing diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: One planner-owned artifact or documented equivalent owns exact-key proof.
Admission, EXPLAIN, cache, and execution consume the same artifact.
Invalid cases do not become generic scan fallbacks.
findings.suggested_patch_prompt: Tighten 0.197 proof ownership by introducing or documenting a single planner-owned exact-primary-key canonicalization artifact and updating admission/explain/cache tests to prove all consumers use it.

evidence: Empty semantic tests exist.
No complete zero data-store get/index range/row decode tests found for all contradictory exact-key shapes.

pk IN () reports Empty with zero IO.
findings.suggested_patch_prompt: Add zero-IO Empty proof tests for contradictory exact-primary-key predicates and adjust planning so excluded exact-key intersections select Empty rather than fetching a key then filtering it out.
